title: Wire ntm activity (not health) into pane-state checks; add frozen-spinner detector
description:
## Problem
Today skillos:0.1 was wedged in codex 'Working (4m 04s • esc to interrupt)' state with a queued '› Summarize recent commits' prompt unsubmitted. The orchestrator did not catch this because flywheel scripts call 'ntm health' for pane state, which classifies the pane as idle (matches '›' as idle_prompt without checking spinner above).

ntm activity --json correctly reports state=THINKING with velocity=0 and duration=0s. We have detection — we just consume the wrong probe.

## Trauma class
codex-queued-not-submitted-undetected. Earlier session instance: 02:14Z meat-puppet cluster.

## Fix
1. Update _shared/pane-state.sh (and any callers in /flywheel:status, /flywheel:tick Step 3) to consume 'ntm activity --json' as primary truth source. 'ntm health' becomes secondary cross-check only.
2. Add freeze rule: state in [THINKING, GENERATING] AND velocity=0 AND duration > 120s => classify FROZEN_SUSPECT. Surface in /flywheel:status output.
3. Update memory feedback_pane_state_ntm_health.md: correct the rule to 'ntm activity is primary, ntm health is secondary'.

## Acceptance criteria
- _shared/pane-state.sh outputs include state field from activity probe
- /flywheel:status shows FROZEN_SUSPECT row when applicable
- /flywheel:tick Step 3 refuses to dispatch to a FROZEN_SUSPECT pane
- Memory file updated
- Test: synthetic frozen pane (codex Working+queued) classified as FROZEN_SUSPECT

## Cross-references
- Today's recovery (skillos:0.1) used: ntm respawn --panes=1 --force then tmux send-keys 'codex --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox -m gpt-5.5' Enter
- Sister bead: flywheel:recover-pane slash command
- Upstream issue: ntm health vs ntm activity disagreement (file at Dicklesworthstone/ntm)

title: [fleet-onboarding] canonical-cli-scoping process for new-repo onboarding/sync/stamp
description:
Joshua directive 2026-05-03 ~08:05Z: 'we also need a full mapped /canonical-cli-scoping process for onboarding new repos into the flywheel. I have a lot of sessions. We need full scoped onboarding, sync, stamp'

GAP: Current state has no canonical onboarding contract. Each repo (flywheel, skillos, mobile-eats, alps, terra-title, blackfoot, zesttube, zeststream-infra, etc.) was bootstrapped ad-hoc. Result: skillos LIMPING for weeks before tonight's diagnostic; mobile-eats receipt-mirror gap discovered manually 2026-05-03; INCIDENTS distribution gap (flywheel-rx1t) discovered manually. Fleet member contract is implicit, so repos drift into LIMPING by default.

MEADOWS #5 (RULES): Define canonical-cli-scoping contract that EVERY fleet-managed repo MUST satisfy for HEALTHY signals from day 1. Make the implicit explicit, mechanically enforced.

PROPOSED canonical onboarding surface (scoped per canonical-cli-scoping skill):

  /flywheel:onboard <repo-path> [options]
    --dry-run           # full scope check, no mutations
    --doctor            # health probe of existing onboarding
    --info              # what onboarding does + checklist
    --schema            # JSON schema of onboarding contract
    --examples          # sample onboarded-repo inventory
    --json              # machine-readable result
    --stamp             # write canonical stamp to repo
    --sync              # re-sync existing repo to latest fleet-member contract
    --upgrade           # apply latest fleet-member doctrine bumps
    --explain           # show what each step would do + why
    --idempotency-key   # safe re-run

ONBOARDING CONTRACT (required for HEALTHY):
  Tier 1 — Files (substrate):
    [ ] .flywheel/MISSION.md (with mission anchor)
    [ ] .flywheel/GOAL.md (current goal locked)
    [ ] .flywheel/STATE.md (active state)
    [ ] AGENTS.md (canonical L-rules synced from fleet)
    [ ] INCIDENTS.md (selected canonical entries — solves flywheel-rx1t)
    [ ] .flywheel/dispatch-log.jsonl (worker callback log)
    [ ] .flywheel/ticks/ (receipt directory)

  Tier 2 — Loop driver (mobile-eats-pattern, addresses Joshua escalation tonight):
    [ ] launchd plist registered for canonical loop fire
    [ ] product-specific work payload script (NOT just /flywheel:tick)
    [ ] ledger write path (canonical mirror via flywheel-1naj.1 pattern)
    [ ] receipt write path → ~/.local/state/flywheel-loop/last_tick_<project>.json
    [ ] callback contract → flywheel orchestrator pane via L61

  Tier 3 — Session topology:
    [ ] ntm session created with declared roles
    [ ] session-topology.jsonl entry (orchestrator_pane, callback_pane, worker_panes, human_pane)
    [ ] FoggyBear-equivalent vault token if cross-session L61 needed
    [ ] Agent Mail identity registered per pane

  Tier 4 — Health probes (the doctor surface):
    [ ] flywheel-loop doctor PASS
    [ ] gap-hunter HEALTHY (5/5 loop-integrity signals — flywheel-aucl)
    [ ] mission-lock fresh
    [ ] pane states all reportable via --robot-activity

  Tier 5 — Fleet membership stamp:
    [ ] .flywheel/fleet-member.json with: onboarded_at, contract_version, doctrine_hash, last_sync_at
    [ ] Registered in ~/.local/state/flywheel/fleet-roster.json
    [ ] Daily-sync subscription (gets doctrine drift, INCIDENTS additions, AGENTS.md L-rule updates)

ACCEPTANCE GATES:
  [ ] /flywheel:onboard skill authored at ~/.claude/skills/flywheel/onboard/ (or as /flywheel:onboard slash command)
  [ ] passes canonical-cli-scoping/scripts/check-cli-scoping.sh
  [ ] dry-run mode validated against existing flywheel + mobile-eats (should report 'already-onboarded')
  [ ] dry-run validated against skillos (should report tier-2 gaps matching tonight's diagnostic)
  [ ] dry-run validated against a fresh disposable test repo (should report all 5 tiers missing)
  [ ] stamp mode applied to skillos brings it to HEALTHY within 24h
  [ ] sync mode propagates AGENTS.md + canonical INCIDENTS to existing fleet members
  [ ] daily-sync wires into skillos's new mobile-eats-pattern loop (compose with flywheel-b6js)

DEPENDENCIES:
  - flywheel-aucl (loop-integrity gap class — DONE, defines tier 4 health gate)
  - flywheel-b6js (skillos mobile-eats-pattern — needed for tier 2 daily-sync delivery mechanism)
  - flywheel-rx1t (INCIDENTS distribution from init — RESOLVED by tier 1 stamp + tier 5 daily-sync)
  - flywheel-1naj.1 (mobile-eats receipt mirror — DONE, defines tier 2 ledger contract)
  - flywheel-ic6 (L69 + L70 commits — extend to L71 fleet-member-contract)

CANONICAL-CLI-SCOPING REFERENCE: ~/.claude/skills/canonical-cli-scoping/SKILL.md

DOCTRINE LINKS:
  - mobile-eats = HEALTHY proof pattern (from loop-integrity diagnostic tonight)
  - skillos = LIMPING anti-pattern (4 dead signals out of 5 caught tonight)
  - feedback_wire_into_ecosystem META-RULE
  - feedback_two_truth_sources_before_decide

ANTI-PATTERN AVOIDED: ad-hoc onboarding produces ad-hoc fleet members. Joshua flagged 'I have a lot of sessions' — that scale demands mechanical enforcement, not memorable checklists.

WHEN TO ACTIVATE: design phase can start NOW (after tonight's wave 2/3 application landings free workers). Implementation should follow once skillos mobile-eats-pattern lands so we have a SECOND HEALTHY proof to validate against.

PHASING:
  Phase 1: Author canonical CLI surface (--help/--info/--schema/--examples/--doctor/--dry-run/--json) — design only
  Phase 2: Implement contract validation (the 5 tiers as probes)
  Phase 3: Implement stamp + sync + upgrade operations  
  Phase 4: Validate against 3 existing repos + 1 fresh disposable
  Phase 5: Roll out to fleet roster (cross-repo doctrine sync via PostToolUse hook)

Current proof: 6/16 collector health, 4/8 fleet repo coverage, ledger growth=2; status degraded but usable as a HEALTHY-signal input, not a hard gate.

title: Implement L66 data-backed deferral guard
description:
## Goal
Implement the data-backed dispatch deferral guard designed by flywheel-28v so orchestrators stop asking Joshua when evidence already selects a safe action.

## Context
PHASE: L66 implementation follow-up
CRITERION: autonomy-guardrail
Doctor in /Users/josh/Developer/skillos surfaced trauma_class=orchestrator-asked-instead-of-decided at /Users/josh/.local/state/flywheel/fuckup-log.jsonl#L185. The row says Phase 2 REFINE r1 had 5 Joshua-disposes, all recommended yes, with 3552 lines / 3 lanes / 5+ alignments, but the orchestrator asked for blanket approval instead of declaring the decisions and proceeding. L66 is already canonical and points to flywheel-28v as the durable target; flywheel-28v closed with plan-space design only, so this bead is the implementation owner.

## Dependencies
Depends on: flywheel-28v closed design artifact /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/plans/data-backed-deferral-guard-2026-05-01.md. Related: flywheel-8i5 dispatch enforcement design.

## Inputs / Outputs
INPUTS: flywheel-28v design, AGENTS.md L66, INCIDENTS.md meat-puppet/orchestrator decision incidents, fuckup-log row 185, dispatch-log/session topology/ready-work signals.
OUTPUTS: hook, tick check, or dispatch-skill guard that detects question-shaped deferral when data supports a concrete next action; fixtures proving allowed ambiguity remains allowed.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Detector catches question-shaped deferral phrasing when the L66 four-check gate passes: idle capacity, ready work, prior wave data points one way, and action is in doctrine.
- Detector emits structured violation class orch_meat_puppet_question_with_sufficient_data or a compatible L66 class with the missing evidence fields included.
- Genuine ambiguity or missing data must pass only when the output names the specific missing datum or tie.
- Regression fixture covers the 2026-05-02 row: 5 Joshua-disposes, all recommended yes, cross-lane convergence, and an ask-for-blanket-approval output.
- No raw pane transport is introduced; any pane probes use ntm surfaces.

## Testing Obligations
- Unit tests for pass/fail L66 four-check gate fixtures.
- Smoke or dry-run that shows the guard emits a SOFT violation without mutating source state.
- br dep cycles --json remains empty.

## Definition of Done
COMMIT: fix(flywheel): implement L66 deferral guard [flywheel-XXXX]
- Tests green
- AUTONOMY: autonomous

title: [skillos-fleet-mail-notify-gap] Wire routed decision notices
description:
## Goal
Wire skillos routed-decision notifications so `needs_fleet_mail` rows reach flywheel through L61/L65 dual-channel delivery.

## Context
PHASE: skillos goal-rotation substrate repair
CRITERION: flagship-anchor-skillos
Dispatch `/tmp/dispatch_skillos-meadows-goal-rotation_2026_05_03.md` found 5 rows in `~/.local/state/flywheel/skillos-routed.jsonl`, all stuck at `notification_status=needs_fleet_mail` since 2026-05-03T03:44:34Z. Topology has skillos `fleet_mail_identity=FoggyBear` and flywheel `fleet_mail_identity=LavenderGlen`; LavenderGlen has a token in the fleet-mail vault, but FoggyBear currently has metadata only and no sender token vaulted. Previous LavenderGlen -> FoggyBear intake worked; the missing path is skillos -> flywheel.

## Dependencies
None. Must precede claims that routed-decision notification loop is healthy.

## Inputs / Outputs
INPUTS: `/tmp/skillos-meadows-rotation_findings.md`, `/tmp/skillos-state-v2-update-draft.md`, `~/.local/state/flywheel/skillos-routed.jsonl`, `~/.local/state/flywheel/session-topology.jsonl`, `~/.local/state/flywheel/fleet-mail-tokens/`, L61/L65 doctrine.
OUTPUTS: token-safe sender auth or relay primitive, paired fleet-mail + ntm poke delivery, updated notification statuses or blocked-with-owner receipts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Authenticate or relay as skillos fleet-mail identity without exposing tokens in pane text.
- Send notification for all 5 current routed rows, grouped if appropriate.
- Pair every fleet-mail send with an ntm poke containing message id and project key.
- Update each routed row to `notified` or `blocked_with_owner` with evidence.
- Add a regression guard so future `needs_fleet_mail` rows cannot linger silently beyond one cadence window.

## Testing Obligations
- Run token-vault doctor or equivalent redacted probe.
- Prove recipient fetch path works without echoing tokens.
- Validate JSONL after status updates.
- SKILLS: agent-mail, agent-security, beads-workflow.

## Definition of Done
COMMIT: feat(skillos): wire routed-decision fleet-mail notifications [bead-id]
- Routed rows drained or explicitly blocked.
- L61/L65 dual-channel evidence captured.
- AUTONOMY: autonomous, except any Joshua-required token surfacing is explicit.
notes:
FoggyBear vault audit 2026-05-03T06:21Z:
- skillos topology declares fleet_mail_identity=FoggyBear.
- Legacy metadata envelope exists at ~/.local/state/flywheel/agent-mail-tokens/foggybear.json, mode 0600, with keys identity_id/name/project_key/token.
- Canonical fleet-mail vault file ~/.local/state/flywheel/fleet-mail-tokens/FoggyBear.token is absent.
- LavenderGlen canonical token exists; previous flywheel->skillos intake works, but the current required direction is skillos->flywheel sender auth.

Recommended path:
1. Validate the legacy FoggyBear token without echoing it: load from file into env or stdin-only helper, then run a redacted self/whoami/fetch probe.
2. If valid, migrate to canonical vault format via fleet-mail-token-save.sh or equivalent token-safe helper; never print token in pane text.
3. Send grouped notification for the 5 skillos-routed rows via fleet-mail project as FoggyBear to LavenderGlen, then pair with ntm poke per L61/L65.
4. Update each routed row from needs_fleet_mail to notified or blocked_with_owner with message id / project key evidence.

Joshua-decision fallback options:
- If legacy token invalid: register fresh FoggyBear or a new skillos fleet identity, then update topology and vault.
- If sender auth remains risky: implement a token-safe relay primitive that lets flywheel LavenderGlen send the durable copy while skillos emits a signed/redacted relay request.
close_reason: FoggyBear canonical vault file created with 0600 perms; token-safe smoke message id 41 validated; 5 routed decisions notified in fleet-mail msg id 42 and paired with ntm poke; append-only status rows written to skillos-routed.jsonl.
